Name: Tool-mode split and lean-focus contract
Primary purpose: Define audit-full, phase-check, and lean-focus modes so routine Lean work consumes compact implementation packets instead of full PDF/table scans.
Load-control rule: Full PDF regeneration is not part of ordinary Lean coding; use compact packets unless a governance or audit phase explicitly requests audit-full.
Theory semantics: No theory classification yet; this phase only fixes the mode boundary.
Generated artifacts: workflow-policy row; scaling-plan TSV/TeX; no Lean code

Name: Implementation Packet Generator
Primary purpose: Generate the phase-local packet with goal, source objects, producer/consumer modules, red blockers, yellow risks, forbidden shortcuts, expected artifacts, and comparison-only warnings.
Load-control rule: Packet should fit on a small number of pages/rows and link to full TSV/JSON exports rather than embedding full inventories.
Theory semantics: Consumes module-theory/end-theory ledgers once available; before then marks theory context as unknown-not-input.
Lean-data effect: none unless the user explicitly edits Lean code afterwards

Name: Incremental Lean inventory and file-hash cache
Primary purpose: Avoid rescanning/rendering everything by caching file hashes, module declarations, import edges, and symbol-reference candidates between Lean-data updates.
Load-control rule: Only changed Lean files and dependent generated summaries are refreshed; full inventory rebuild remains available as audit-full.
Theory semantics: May infer rough local background categories from imports, but must mark them heuristic until semantically confirmed.
Generated artifacts: file-hash cache; declaration cache; inventory-delta TSV
Lean-data effect: changes only when Lean files or frozen inventory data change

Name: Module theory context ledger
Primary purpose: Track local established mathematical background per module as formal language, proof method, library/API context, implementation substrate, or comparison lemma context.
Load-control rule: Start with active/relevant modules only; do not manually annotate all modules at once. Python proposes rows from imports; semantic closure is explicit and phase-bound.
Theory semantics: Local established theory is allowed formalization context, never ontic CNNA generator. Examples: Matrix, Finset, Polynomial, graph/linear algebra, Schur-style algebraic methods.

Name: End-theory comparison ledger
Primary purpose: Track large established target theories as comparison-only horizons: AQFT/QFT, OQS, modular forms, CM, elliptic functions/curves, L-functions, Moonshine, renormalization, operator algebras, spectral geometry, etc.
Load-control rule: Only record end theories that constrain current or near-future phases; do not build encyclopedia entries.
Theory semantics: End theories are target structures, consistency checks, terminology alignment, or obstruction/comparison mirrors; forbidden as CNNA producers.

Name: Active object graph slices
Primary purpose: Render small producer-consumer/provenance neighborhoods around the active phase and active CNNA objects rather than dumping the full object/declaration graph into the PDF.
Load-control rule: Depth 1-2 graph slices only in PDF; full graph exported as DOT/JSON for optional audit.
Theory semantics: Graph edges distinguish internal provenance, local formalization context, and external comparison-only references.
Lean-data effect: none unless generated from changed Lean inventory

Name: Red-blocker addressing-phase generator
Primary purpose: When the pre-implementation thought experiment marks a future phase red, propose an upstream addressing phase at the earliest natural source layer before the blocked phase becomes active.
Load-control rule: Generate concise phase proposals only for red blockers; yellow risks remain notes in the implementation packet.
Theory semantics: If the blocker is an imported end-theory assumption, route to internal derivation/comparison-boundary phase rather than patching the consumer.
Generated artifacts: red-blocker phase-proposal TSV; accepted phase rows only after approval
Lean-data effect: none until an accepted phase later changes Lean code

Name: Database-ready YAML schema and review-link normalization
Primary purpose: Normalize IDs, phase/object/proof-dossier references, review statuses, and theory-reference markers so the YAML master can later be exported to relational or graph database tables without semantic loss.
Load-control rule: Do not manually annotate the whole repository at once. Start with active/near-active rows and generated consistency checks; keep full database realization as a later export task.
Theory semantics: Database fields are review metadata. They may mark local background theory or end-theory comparison context, but they do not create CNNA generators.
Generated artifacts: database-readiness checklist TSV; normalized ID/reference audit; review-status export slice

Name: Database export, round-trip, and interactive documentation scaffold
Primary purpose: Prepare future YAML-to-DB exports, database-to-generated-view round-trip checks, interactive object/phase exploration, online documentation, and discipline-separated expert-review views.
Tool mode: audit-full optional plus generated export layer
Load-control rule: Keep export scripts reproducible and optional. Lean implementation packets must remain compact and must not depend on full database exploration.
Theory semantics: Interactive views are secondary/generated. They expose review obligations and comparison horizons but never certify semantic truth.
Generated artifacts: future SQL/SQLite/graph export scripts; schema diagrams; interactive-view JSON; round-trip consistency report
Lean-data effect: none unless future scripts read changed Lean inventory data

Name: Context documentation slices
Primary purpose: Reduce documentation overhead by producing topic/phase-specific packets that preserve full section topology while filtering rows to the requested context.
Tool mode: Python context generator over YAML and generated TSV exports; optional pdflatex compilation.
Load-control rule: Use for targeted requests and phase closeouts; reserve full PDF regeneration for tool releases, global audits, or explicit full-document requests.
Theory semantics: Generated-secondary navigation/review view only. Absence from a slice is not proof of semantic irrelevance; expert review and blue/reference audit obligations remain.
Lean-data effect: None; no Lean source mutation and no Lean build claim.

Name: Stats front section and interface-metaphor documentation surface
Primary purpose: Keep project-scale facts, repository inventory, graph views, license notices and the tool-interface Leitmetapher visible without creating another truth layer.
Load-control rule: Do not expand the opening section into prose-heavy explanation; keep it as compact facts, concise metaphor, licensing note and existing inventory/graph inclusion.
Theory semantics: Generated-secondary review architecture only. The metaphor is operational, not ontic or theorematic; Lean-primary and expert-review rules remain dominant.
Generated artifacts: project_stats.generated.tsv; tables/project_stats_table.tex; tables/stats.tex; updated V1.09 full PDF.
Lean-data effect: none; no Lean source or Lean-data timestamp change.

Name: Root artifact retention and disposable documentation outputs
Primary purpose: Keep the project root clean and source-oriented while preserving on-demand generation of full PDFs/TeX and archival access to historical reports.
Tool mode: Python full-document generator plus root cleanup script.
Load-control rule: Do not package regenerated full PDFs/TeX by default. Generate them only for review/export tasks, then keep them in generated_docs/full/ or delete them before release packaging.
Theory semantics: Generated documentation outputs remain generated-secondary. Archival Markdown reports are trace records, not authoritative theory sources.
Generated artifacts: generated_docs/full/<version>.tex/pdf/log when explicitly generated; archive/reports/*.md for historical reports; generated_exports/root_artifact_cleanup.generated.json when cleanup runs.
Lean-data effect: none; no Lean source mutation and no Lean build claim.

Name: Root license and PDF credit surface
Primary purpose: Keep license, editor/assistant credits and minimum tested ChatGPT workflow requirements visible without creating duplicate documentation entry points.
Tool mode: Root README/LICENSE plus generator metadata rendering for full/context PDFs.
Load-control rule: Do not duplicate README files in subdirectories; keep license and credit text compact and generated-secondary.
Theory semantics: Licensing/credits are governance metadata only. They do not affect CNNA derivations, Lean truth status, or expert-review obligations.
Generated artifacts: Updated license_notice.generated.tsv; tables/license_notice.tex; generated full/context PDFs with author credits when explicitly compiled.
Lean-data effect: none; no Lean source mutation and no Lean build claim.

Name: provider-neutral SQL database public read-only mirror and cnna-project.net frontend sidechain
Primary purpose: Prepare a public query/visualization layer for CNNA planning metadata without replacing Lean/YAML truth and without public write authority.
Tool mode: SQL/database mirror schemas and read-only api views; local importer; future static/web frontend; no live DB connection in V1.22.
Load-control rule: Start as schema/import/view contracts, not as full web application. No PDFs/ZIPs in DB, no admin write credential key in browser/chat, no manual production table edits.
Theory semantics: Database/web layer is generated-secondary/publication infrastructure. It can query, visualize and review Lean/YAML-derived metadata, but cannot generate CNNA axioms or release Lean phases.
Generated artifacts: Future SQL migrations, import reports, snapshot checksums, read-only access policy test reports, frontend data-contract docs and public views; V1.22 adds planning records only.
Lean-data effect: none; no Lean source mutation and no Lean build claim.

Name: GitHub backup-gated CI snapshot automation bridge
Primary purpose: Prepare a safe automated path from repository commit to Lean build, inventory scan and versioned snapshot artifacts without direct unprotected main mutation.
Tool mode: GitHub Actions plus backup tag/branch and work-branch/PR policy; no live repository mutation in P1.37.
Load-control rule: Begin with contracts and dry-run artifacts; do not automate merge/deploy until branch protection, backup refs, manifests and CI failure behavior are tested.
Theory semantics: CI status is formal/build evidence only. Green does not imply semantic infallibility, and artifacts are generated-secondary.
Generated artifacts: Future workflow logs, snapshot_manifest.json, YAML/JSON/TSV/SQL artifacts, commit/toolchain/hash manifests and CI status badges/reports.
Lean-data effect: none in P1.37; future CI may build Lean but must not change Lean source unless a separate PR does so.

Name: Webhosting MySQL staging import, read-only swap and rollback bridge
Primary purpose: Prepare the practical classic Webhosting/MySQL deployment path for the CNNA Explorer while preserving strict read-only public access and rollback.
Tool mode: Server-local importer, staging/current table swap, least-privilege DB users, snapshot validation and rollback scripts; no live DB connection in P1.37.
Load-control rule: Import only generated snapshot payloads; validate staging before swap; keep previous current snapshot and SQL dump until new snapshot passes review.
Theory semantics: MySQL is a presentation mirror and audit surface, not CNNA truth. Manual DB edits cannot release phases or define objects.
Generated artifacts: Future SQL migrations, importer logs, validation reports, read-only access tests, rollback reports, database dumps and local archive manifests.
Lean-data effect: none; no Lean source mutation and no Lean build claim.
